metadata.title: Portrait
metadata.note: Dataset compiled from a public-domain text. Coordinates via OpenStreetMap/Nominatim; routes via OSRM (driving) / BRouter (foot).

properties.name: Clongowes Wood College
properties.gloss: Stephen's first boarding school; the square ditch, the infirmary, and the unjust pandybatting by Father Dolan.
properties.quote: Once upon a time and a very good time it was there was a moocow coming down along the road and this moocow that was coming down along the road met a nicens little boy named baby tuckoo.

properties.name: The Dedalus home, Bray
properties.gloss: The bitter Christmas-dinner quarrel over Parnell between Dante, Mr Casey and Simon Dedalus.

properties.name: Blackrock
properties.gloss: The family's home in faded prosperity; Stephen's Monte Cristo daydreams and evening runs with Mike Flynn.

properties.name: Belvedere College
properties.gloss: Stephen's Jesuit day school in the city; the Whitsuntide play and his growing inward estrangement.

properties.name: Cork
properties.gloss: Stephen travels with his father to sell the last of the family property; he feels utterly estranged.

properties.name: Queen's College, Cork
properties.gloss: In his father's old anatomy theatre the word ‘Foetus’ cut into a desk jolts Stephen with shame.

properties.name: Nighttown
properties.gloss: The brothel quarter (Monto); at the chapter's close Stephen wanders into the arms of a prostitute.

properties.name: The retreat, Belvedere College
properties.gloss: The three-day retreat and the terrifying sermons on death and hell that drive Stephen to repentance.

properties.name: Church Street chapel
properties.gloss: Across the city, in terror of damnation, Stephen makes his confession to a Capuchin priest.

properties.name: Bull Wall, Dollymount
properties.gloss: The wading girl — Stephen's great epiphany of beauty and his calling to art rather than priesthood.
properties.quote: A girl stood before him in midstream, alone and still, gazing out to sea. She seemed like one whom magic had changed into the likeness of a strange and beautiful seabird.

properties.name: North Bull Island
properties.gloss: Stephen crosses the wooden bridge to the strand where, wading, he sees the bird-girl.

properties.name: The director's office, Belvedere
properties.gloss: The director sounds Stephen on a vocation to the priesthood — an offer he silently declines.

properties.name: University College Dublin (Newman House)
properties.gloss: Stephen the university student; the dean of studies, the aesthetic theory, the villanelle.

properties.name: National Library of Ireland
properties.gloss: On its steps Stephen watches the birds and argues with Cranly about belief and exile.

properties.name: St Stephen's Green
properties.gloss: The Green Stephen crosses daily, emblem of the city he is preparing to leave.

properties.name: Kingstown Harbour (departure)
properties.gloss: The diary's close: Stephen resolves to leave Ireland for the continent.
properties.quote: Welcome, O life! I go to encounter for the millionth time the reality of experience and to forge in the smithy of my soul the uncreated conscience of my race.
